note: Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/Darboux.lean:30 — theorem exists_hasDerivWithinAt_eq_of_gt_of_lt: f' a < m < f' b implies m in f' image of (a,b); file docstring: 'Darboux's theorem' proving the derivative takes all intermediate values, matching the analysis-sense Wikipedia/nLab article (not the symplectic-geometry Darboux theorem).
verify_note: Decl is real (Darboux.lean:30, confirmed against the mathlib4 checkout) and does prove the analysis-sense Darboux theorem, but Q1058662 itself is the WRONG QID: its Wikidata description is 'foundational result in symplectic geometry' and its enwiki sitelink is the symplectic/differential-geometry 'Darboux's theorem' article, not the real-analysis one. The correct QID for 'the derivative has the intermediate value property' is the separate item Q660799 (description 'theorem in real analysis', enwiki 'Darboux's theorem (analysis)'). The proposal's own parenthetical disclaiming the symplectic sense is right in spirit but cites exactly that wrong QID — overclaimed 'exact' match on the wrong concept.

note: Mathlib/NumberTheory/Primorial.lean:40 — def primorial (n : ℕ) : ℕ := ∏ p ∈ range (n+1) with p.Prime, p — the product of primes ≤ n, exactly the primorial function.
verify_note: Decl confirmed in the cache (kind def, Primorial.html#primorial) and in the mathlib4 checkout at exactly line 40 with the body quoted verbatim in the note; Q1071077's Wikidata label 'primorial' / description 'integer representing the product of the first n prime numbers' / enwiki 'Primorial' is precisely this object — 'exact' is an honest, non-overclaimed match_kind.
